title: GrimoireV3Enums
description: Shared enum vocabularies for v3.0 training data

power_source.description: What gives one character power over another

want_type.description: What one character wants from another

underlying_conflict.description: The deeper issue beneath surface disagreements

obstacle_type.description: Category of what prevents character from achieving objective

tactic.description: How a character pursues their objective

violation_signature.description: How Gricean maxim violations manifest in dialogue

wound_category.description: Category of core wound (not specific character wound)

social_role.description: Social role character inhabits in scene

setting_type.description: Category of setting location (synced with enums/setting.json)

prop_category.description: Category of physical object in scene

character_role.description: Unified character role for scene references (not slug)

scene_polarity.description: Dramatic polarity for scene turn tracking

speech_register.description: Level of formality in character speech

perception_of_other.description: What one character expects from another (creates 'The Gap')

trigger_type.description: What activates a character's wound response

relationship_turn.description: How relationship state changes in a scene

emotional_arc.description: How character's emotional state changes through scene

personal_arc_beat.description: Snyder beat for character's personal arc (not plot)

objective_object.description: What type of thing is being acted upon

objective_constraint.description: How the objective must be achieved

time_of_day.description: Time of day for scene setting

psychic_distance.description: Gardner psychic distance level: 1=distant narrator, 2=close narrator, 3=neutral filter, 4=deep filter (character's voice), 5=stream of consciousness

personal_stakes.description: What's at stake for the character personally

callback_thread_type.description: What kind of callback this is

information_asymmetry.description: Knowledge differential between reader and character

gap_domain.description: What domain the information gap concerns

motif_deployment.description: How motif is used in scene

objective_correlative_type.description: What external element carries emotional meaning

controlling_idea_position.description: How scene relates to novel's controlling idea

dominant_social_circle.description: Which social circle dominates the scene

territory_type.description: Whose territory the scene takes place in

knowledge_domain.description: What category of thing is known

sentence_length_category.description: Sentence length: fragment (<5), short (5-12), medium (13-25), long (26-50), marathon (>50 words)

prose_rhythm.description: Rhythm pattern: staccato (short bursts), flowing (long unbroken), varied (contrasting lengths), periodic (main clause delayed), telegraphic (extreme brevity), cumulative (building detail)

figure_type.description: Figurative language device (Jakobson axis of selection)

defamiliarization_level.description: Shklovsky ostranenie: automatized (transparent), mild (slight friction), moderate (noticeable strangeness), strong (perception forced), radical (language foregrounded)

process_type.description: Halliday transitivity: material (doing), mental (sensing), verbal (saying), relational (being), behavioral (physiological), existential (existing)

discourse_mode.description: Mode of representing speech and thought (Cohn/Leech & Short)

foregrounding_type.description: Leech & Short foregrounding: external_deviation (from language norms), internal_deviation (from text's own pattern), parallelism (repeated pattern), cohesion_pattern (linking), semantic_saturation (word field dominance)

prose_pacing.description: Scene vs. summary ratio at prose level

vocabulary_source.description: Whose vocabulary appears in FID passages

clause_position.description: Position of main clause within sentence

inciting_incident_type.description: Type of inciting incident that forces collision into motion

drive_model.description: Character motivation model: wound (Weiland/Cron), desire (wants-based), duty (obligation-based), perception (epistemology-based), existential (meaning-based)

opposition_level.description: Level at which antagonist opposes protagonist (Truby)

diegetic_level.description: Genette narrative level: extradiegetic (outer frame), intradiegetic (main story), metadiegetic (story-within-story)

illocutionary_force.description: Specific illocutionary force of speech act (Austin/Searle)

power_asymmetry_type.description: Power balance type in collision pattern

narrator_reliability_type.description: Booth/Nünning narrator reliability: reliable, factually (misreports), interpretively (misinterprets), evaluatively (misjudges), self-deceptive (believes own distortions), culturally_contingent (reliability depends on reader frames)

knowledge_gap_source.description: Sternberg: what creates the information gap — withheld_past (curiosity), uncertain_future (suspense), suppressed_by_focalizer (dramatic irony)

trauma_representation_mode.description: Caruth/Herman/LaCapra: how trauma is narratively represented — integrated (processed), fragmented (shattered), belated (returns later), acting_out (compulsive repetition), working_through (conscious integration)

somatic_trauma_response.description: Van der Kolk embodied trauma: body-first responses before cognition

metafictional_technique.description: Waugh/McHale metafiction: techniques that foreground fiction's constructed nature

metaphor_quality.description: Max Black interaction theory: dead (lost force), conventional (familiar), active (genuine tension), resonant (radiates meaning), mixed (multiple vehicles)

want_need_alignment.description: Truby want vs. need: aligned (want=need), opposed (want blocks need), unknown_need (character unaware), false_want (pursuing wrong goal), evolving (relationship shifting)

burke_form.description: Burke form taxonomy: progressive (builds stepwise), repetitive (recurring pattern), conventional (genre expectation), minor (local flourish)

anti_mimetic_technique.description: Richardson unnatural narratology: anti-mimetic narrative techniques

actantial_role.$comment: DEPRECATED: use actant instead. Kept for backwards compatibility.

irony_type.description: Booth/Hutcheon irony taxonomy: verbal (says opposite), situational (events contradict expectations), dramatic (audience knows more), structural (sustained ironic distance), cosmic (fate mocks), socratic (feigned ignorance), romantic (author breaks frame)

gaze_type.description: Mulvey gaze theory: active (perceives/acts), passive_spectacle (looked at), scopophilic (visual pleasure), power_gaze (surveillance/domination), counter_gaze (returned/resisted), neutral (unmarked)

transtextuality_type.description: Genette transtextuality: intertextuality (quotation/allusion), paratextuality (titles/prefaces), metatextuality (commentary), hypertextuality (transformation), architextuality (genre relation)

comic_mode.description: Bergson/Morreall comedy modes: satire (critique), manners (social hypocrisy), farce (physical chaos), dark_comedy (suffering as humor), absurdist (logic fails), picaresque (outsider exposes), romantic_comedy (love through misunderstanding), deadpan (flat delivery)

textual_affect_type.description: Ahmed/Berlant affect theory: cruel_optimism (attachment to damaging object), affective_stickiness (emotion adheres to objects), ugly_feelings (envy/anxiety/irritation vs. grand emotions), slow_death (attrition under capitalism), impasse (suspension of agency), disorientation (loss of bearings)

anti_mimetic_technique.description: Richardson unnatural narratology: impossible_narrator (dead/unborn/omniscient-first-person), denarration (events un-happen), contradictory_storyworld (logical impossibilities), anti_temporal (reversed/scrambled causality), permutation (multiple incompatible versions), second_person_anti_mimetic (you-as-protagonist defamiliarization)

event_significance.description: Chatman story/discourse: kernel (plot-changing, cannot be removed without altering story logic), satellite (texture, elaboration, can be removed without changing plot)

jakobson_function.description: Jakobson six functions of language: referential (context/world), emotive (speaker attitude), conative (effect on addressee), phatic (contact/connection), metalingual (about language itself), poetic (message form)

narratee_type.description: Prince narratee theory: explicit (named/addressed directly), implicit (constructed by narration but unnamed), dramatized (narratee as character with reactions), zero_degree (unmarked/universal reader position)

pentad_focus.description: Burke Pentad: which element dominates — act (what happened), scene (where/when), agent (who did it), agency (how/by what means), purpose (why)

emplotment_type.description: Hayden White emplotment: romance (hero transcends world), tragedy (fall from grace), comedy (reconciliation with society), satire (inadequacy of consciousness)

burke_form_type.description: Burke form types: progressive (syllogistic/qualitative/plot-driven), repetitive (variations on theme), conventional (genre expectations), minor (local effects like metaphor)

freudian_mechanism.description: Freud: symbol/dream-work mechanisms — condensation (multiple meanings in one image), displacement (affect shifted to safer target), uncanny (familiar made strange), repetition_compulsion (re-enacting trauma), projection/rationalization/sublimation/repression (defense mechanisms)

lacan_register.description: Lacan three registers: real (pre-linguistic, traumatic, unsymbolizable), symbolic (language, law, social order), imaginary (image, ego, mirror-stage identification)

abject_category.description: Kristeva abjection: corporeal (bodily waste), maternal (repudiated origin), religious_defilement (taboo/pollution), death (corpse as ultimate abject), identity_threat (category disturbance)

mimesis_phase.description: Ricoeur threefold mimesis: prefiguration (pre-understanding of action/symbolic field), configuration (emplotment/text creation), refiguration (reader reception/lived application)

postcolonial_mode.description: Said/Spivak/Bhabha: orientalism (exoticizing), hybridity (cultural mixing), third_space (in-between), mimicry (colonial imitation), double_consciousness (Du Bois/Fanon), subaltern_silence (Spivak), decolonial (resistance), ambivalence (desire/dread), structural_dehumanization (Achebe), language_politics (Ngũgĩ)

feminist_narrative_type.description: Lanser/DuPlessis/Warhol/Fetterley: engaging/distancing narrator (Warhol), marriage_plot vs. writing_beyond_ending (DuPlessis), immasculation (Fetterley), authority_and_gender + voice modes (Lanser)

queer_mode.description: Sedgwick/Butler/Edelman/Halberstam/Muñoz: closet_epistemology (knowledge/secrecy), compulsory_heterosexuality (Rich), reproductive_futurism (Edelman), queer_temporality (Halberstam), queer_futurity (Muñoz)

performativity_mode.description: Butler: citationality (sedimented norms), drag (parodic exposure), passing (normative concealment), misfire (failed performance), subversive_repetition (displacing citation), disidentification (Muñoz)

survivance_mode.description: Vizenor: survivance (active presence), victimry (colonial victim construction), trickster_discourse (radical irony/transformation), dead_voices (colonial simulations of Indianness)

storywork_protocol.description: Archibald Indigenous Storywork: 7 principles of ethical engagement with Indigenous stories

narrative_ethics_mode.description: Booth/Keen/Nussbaum/Spivak: coduction (implied author as moral agent), empathy modes (Keen), moral_laboratory (Nussbaum), complicity (reader implication), representation_ethics/power_dynamics (Spivak)

event_type.description: Category of scene turn event. Each value maps to a deterministic verb phrase in the canonical summary template (see summary_template.py). 'dialogue_exchange' is DEPRECATED — use 'dialogue'.

outcome_type.description: Whether the character achieved their scene want

want_outcome.description: Canonical summary: whether the POV character's scene-want was satisfied. Used in the deterministic canonical summary template.

scene_stakes_domain.description: Canonical summary: domain of stakes at risk in this scene. Distinct from personal_stakes (character-level) — this is the scene-level domain category.

causal_role_type.description: How this unit functions causally in the plot. Includes SIP core roles (setup/trigger/complication/resolution), structural connectors (payoff/pivot/bridge), and dramatic beats (catalyst/escalation/crisis/climax/denouement).

role_in_scene.description: Character's functional role in the scene

arc_type.description: Direction of a character's arc through the story

interpreted_value.$comment: GBR wrapper-only form. SIP _base.schema.json defines a full oneOf[plain_value, wrapper] pattern with the same name. This def is the wrapper half only — field schemas use oneOf[<bare_type>, $ref interpreted_value] to compose the full pattern.
interpreted_value.description: Structured wrapper for interpretation fields carrying confidence and provenance metadata. This is the WRAPPER OBJECT form only. Field schemas compose the full pattern as oneOf[<bare_type>, { $ref: interpreted_value }].

value.description: The interpreted value itself (type varies per field)

role.description: Narrative role a character plays in the story (from enums/character.json)

wound.description: Core wound category — umbrella + fine-grained (from enums/character.json)

relationship_type.description: Typed relationship between two entities (from enums/relationship.json)

relationship_dynamic.description: Qualitative dynamic within a relationship (from enums/relationship.json)

structures_of_feeling.description: Williams: dominant, residual, or emergent structure of feeling.

violation_type.description: How a Gricean maxim violation manifests in dialogue. Paired with gricean_maxim to fully specify the violation.

state_type.description: Category of participant state measured in pre/post transition.

audit_flag.description: Named representation audit flags. Replaces free-text overall_flags.

subgenre.description: Specific subgenre classification. Complements genre_type (the coarse category).

tactic_shift_pattern.description: Pattern of tactic change through a scene. Replaces free-text tactic_shift.

continuity_time_elapsed.description: Time elapsed since the previous unit. Discretized from free-text descriptions.

scene_function.description: Structural work this scene does (what it accomplishes dramatically).

syntactic_pattern.description: Named syntactic pattern for signature/avoided constructions in prose analysis.

semantic_field.description: Semantic domain of vocabulary and imagery.

stress_level.description: Character's current stress intensity. 'any' is used in stress_profile tells to indicate a tell that surfaces at all levels.

tell_channel.description: Sensory channel through which a character tell manifests. speech = verbal patterns; body = physical gestures/posture; behavioral = actions/habits; cognitive = thought patterns (only visible to reader via narration).

attachment_pattern.description: Bowlby/Ainsworth attachment style governing close-relationship behavior.

recovery_speed.description: How quickly the character returns to emotional baseline after disruption.

frustration_threshold.description: How much pressure the character absorbs before stress behavior activates.
